mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate compresses equity risk premiums by confirming durable economic expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-GDP-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals tight labor market conditions that boost consumer spending expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.5 percent over a 12-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the federal funds rate and the consumer price index indicate an aggressive monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates and erodes corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain resilience, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive skew when INDPRO rises consecutively for two months, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52 percent over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 500 thousand units signals weakening aggregate demand and construction sector distress, which reduces business investment expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits no predictive power when controlling for concurrent changes in the federal funds rate.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, which sustains household income growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 50 percent of 60-day forward windows, or the cross-product term of payroll growth and unemployment decline shows no significant coefficient in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing present valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in months of rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between stagnant real GDP growth and rising consumer price index indicates stagflationary pressure that erodes real purchasing power and corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction signal predicts positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of observed stagflationary episodes, or the joint effect of GDP stagnation and inflation spikes shows no statistically significant negative impact on equity valuations.
